title: Convert to smaller units (c, pt, qt, & gal)
description: Practice converting a US customary measure of volume to a smaller unit.
topic_path: Convert to smaller units (c, pt, qt, & gal)

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{12}$ quarts $\maroonD{1}$ pint to pints.

First, let's convert $\blueD{12}$ quarts to pints.

$1 \text{ quart} = \greenD{2}\text{ pints}$


$\blueD{12} \text{ quarts} = \blueD{12}\times \greenD{2}=\blueD{24}\text{ pints}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{1}$ pint.

$\blueD{24}\text{ pints}+\maroonD{1}\text{ pint}=25\text{ pints}$

- | | | |
$1$ mile $= 5{,}280$ feet| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ cup $= 8$ fluid ounces|

$1$ yard $= 3$ feet||$1$ pound $= 16$ ounces ||$1$ quart $= 2$ pints|
$1$ foot $= 12$ inches||$1$ ton $= 2{,}000$ pounds || $1$ gallon $= 4$ quarts|

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{5}$ gallons $\maroonD{3}$ quarts to quarts.

First, let's convert $\blueD{5}$ gallons to quarts.

$1 \text{ gallon} = \greenD{4}\text{ quarts}$


$\blueD{5} \text{ gallons} = \blueD{5}\times \greenD{4}=\blueD{20}\text{ quarts}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{3}$ quarts.

$\blueD{20}\text{ quarts}+\maroonD{3}\text{ quarts}=23\text{ quarts}$

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{19}$ pints $\maroonD{1}$ cup to cups.

First, let's convert $\blueD{19}$ pints to cups.

$1 \text{ pint} = \greenD{2}\text{ cups}$


$\blueD{19} \text{ pints} = \blueD{19}\times \greenD{2}=\blueD{38}\text{ cups}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{1}$ cup.

$\blueD{38}\text{ cups}+\maroonD{1}\text{ cup}=39\text{ cups}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

$5$ quarts $=$    [[☃ input-number 7]] pints

$5$ quarts $=$    [[☃ input-number 6]] cups

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, let's convert to pints.

$1 \text{ quart} = \greenD{2}\text{ pints}$

So, $\blueD{5} \text{ quarts} = \blueD{5}\times \greenD{2}=\blueD{10}\text{ pints}$.

Now, let's convert to cups.

$1 \text{ pint} = \maroonD{2}\text{ cups}$

So, $\blueD{10} \text{ pints} = \blueD{10}\times \maroonD{2}=\blueD{20}\text{ cups}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert.**  

$6$ gallons $=$   [[☃ input-number 4]] quarts

$6$ gallons $=$   [[☃ input-number 3]] pints

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
First, let's convert to pints.

$1 \text{ gallon} = \greenD{4}\text{ quarts}$

So, $\blueD{6} \text{ gallons} = \blueD{6}\times \greenD{4}=\blueD{24}\text{ quarts}$.

Now, let's convert to cups.

$1 \text{ quart} = \maroonD{2}\text{ pints}$

So, $\blueD{24} \text{ quarts} = \blueD{24}\times \maroonD{2}=\blueD{48}\text{ pints}$.
